name: Clarion County Community Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank with 5 branches across Pennsylvania offering personal and business banking services, online banking, and various loan products.
description_long: Clarion County Community Bank is a community-focused financial institution operating under FDIC insurance with full backing by the U.S. Government. The bank operates five branch locations across Clarion, New Bethlehem, Rimersburg, Franklin, and Erie, Pennsylvania, positioning itself as a local alternative to larger corporate banks. Founded on the principle of reinvesting deposits back into the local economy rather than moving funds out of state, the bank emphasizes personal service and community commitment as core to its identity. Leadership is provided by President/CEO Travis Heiser. The bank offers comprehensive banking services spanning both personal and business segments. Personal banking includes checking and savings accounts with online access, eStatements, mobile banking, two-way text alerts for fraud protection, and surcharge-free ATM access through the Freedom ATM Alliance and Allpoint Network. The loan portfolio includes personal loans, home mortgage loans (with online application), home equity loans, lines of credit, vehicle loans, and business loans. Business customers have access to dedicated mobile banking through the CCCB Biz App for both Android and Apple platforms, alongside standard business banking services. Clarion County Community Bank differentiates itself through its community bank positioning and emphasis on personalized service. The institution explicitly contrasts itself with corporate banks, highlighting faster decision-making, less red tape, and local reinvestment of customer deposits. The bank advertises a 5-star rating from BauerFinancial, Inc., an established independent bank rating service since 1983. Digital conveniences like eStatements, two-way text alerts, 24/7 mobile banking, and online mortgage applications provide modern accessibility alongside traditional relationship banking. As a traditional community bank, Clarion County Community Bank is well-suited for customers seeking local banking relationships and community-oriented financial services. The main caveat is that as a regional bank with limited branch presence, customers outside the five-location service area would need to rely entirely on digital banking options.
services: Business mobile banking via CCCB Biz App (Android/Apple)
Two-way text alerts for debit card fraud detection
Home mortgage loans with online application
Home equity loans and lines of credit
Surcharge-free ATM access (Freedom Alliance and Allpoint Network)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. Government backing
Five convenient branch locations across Pennsylvania (Clarion, New Bethlehem, Rimersburg, Franklin, Erie)
Surcharge-free ATM access through Freedom ATM Alliance and Allpoint Network nationwide
Two-way text alerts for fraud detection with simple confirmation process
Free mobile banking apps (CCCB Biz App) for business customers on Android and Apple
Online mortgage application process with approval and funding as soon as same day
5-star rating from BauerFinancial, Inc., an independent bank rating authority since 1983
Emphasis on personalized service and relationship banking versus corporate impersonality
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only 5 branch locations in Pennsylvania, excluding most U.S. customers
Website content does not disclose specific product rates, fees, APRs, or minimum balance requirements
No information provided about CD rates, savings account yields, or competitive loan terms
Mobile banking capabilities appear focused on business accounts; personal mobile banking details are limited
As a regional bank, may have less advanced fintech features compared to larger national banks or neobanks
best_for: Residents of Clarion, New Bethlehem, Rimersburg, Franklin, or Erie, Pennsylvania seeking local banking relationships
Small business owners in Western Pennsylvania looking for personalized business banking and loan services
Customers prioritizing community reinvestment and local bank support over corporate banking chains
Borrowers seeking home mortgages with streamlined online application and fast approval timelines
diagnosis: Clarion County Community Bank is best for individuals and small businesses in Western Pennsylvania (Clarion, New Bethlehem, Rimersburg, Franklin, Erie areas) who value personalized service and community reinvestment over national banking chains. The main caveat is that geographic limitations and lack of transparent rate/fee disclosure on the website make it unsuitable for out-of-state customers or those seeking comparative pricing information online.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
